creatures.note: Songbirds and typical temperate forest game, including deer and boars, thrive in the dense understoreys of Harlindon.

flora.description: Harlindon features dense, temperate broadleaf and mixed forests with a strong southern-leaning aromatic understorey.

plants.name: Lowland deciduous woods
plants.note: Mixed forests consisting of oak, ash, poplars, and holly thrive in the mild southern coastal soils.

plants.name: Aromatic coastal shrubs
plants.note: Evergreens and aromatic shrubs cover the coastal hills, creating protective woodland belts.

people.description: Inhabitants are primarily Sindar and Laiquendi Elves who prefer a woodland life, along with some Dwarven coal miners in the east.

list.name: Sindar and Laiquendi of Harlindon
list.description: Forest-dwelling Elves who maintain quiet woodland villages, traditional rituals, and woodcraft.

list.name: Dwarf Miners of Harlindon
list.description: Dwarves operating coal and iron mines on the eastern slopes of the southern Blue Mountains.

land.name: Harlindon Forest Belts
land.description: The dense deciduous and evergreen woods covering the coastal lowlands of South Harlindon.
land.biome_hint: warm-temperate coastal forest with oak, ash, poplar, and holly

regions.notes: South Harlindon represents the rustic, woodland-oriented side of Lindon, in contrast to the high-craft ports of Forlindon. Eerily beautiful remnants of Beleriand, characterized by rugged coastal hills, fertile valleys, and evergreen forests. A light rain, drizzle, or ethereal fog falls on most days of the year, allowing mosses, ferns, firs, and spruce to thrive.
regions.elevation: average: 500 feet. lowest point: Felaigram (sea level at low tide, 2 fathoms below at high tide). highest point: Amon Draug 1,634 feet.
regions.administrative_organization: Círdan rules as a common Lord over the nobility of Forlindon and Harlindon, and his rule is acknowledged as a wise and peaceful one by all parties. Nobles live in pleasant and serene estates, sharing their bounty with their subjects.
regions.military: 2,000 Warriors and Sailors (Forlindon: 800 Warriors/Sailors, 800 Levy. Harlindon: 1,200 Sailors/Warriors, 800 Militia).
regions.symbol: Círdan's Device: A White Pelannun on a Blue Field

regions.name: Ronalindon
regions.supplement: Lindon: The Kingdom of the Elves (fan-made Realm #3003)
regions.description: Ronalindon is a serene coastal subregion of Harlindon situated along the shores of the Gulf of Lhûn and Belegaer. Famed for its peaceful forest glades, quiet river estuaries, and coastal mudflats, it represents a pristine maritime-woodland environment where Elves live in harmony with both the forest and the sea. Geology consists of folded sedimentary rock (slate and sandstone) with rich iron lodes and abundant coal deposits in forlindon.

climate.description: Wet maritime-temperate climate characterized by high humidity, frequent coastal fogs, and ocean breezes. Mean annual temperature: 50-60° F. Average low: Ninui 15° F. Average high: Úrui 75° F. Average annual precipitation: 40-60 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Very mild coastal winters with temperatures rarely dropping below freezing, and cool, misty summers averaging 50-65°F.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Abundant annual rainfall (45–60 inches), with thick morning sea-mists common year-round.
climate.notes: Moist sea air colliding with the coastal hills creates a highly humid microclimate.

fauna.description: Estuarine and coastal species, including migratory waterfowl, marine birds, and forest mammals.

creatures.name: Coastal and river waterfowl
creatures.note: Gulls, herons, and other wading birds thrive in the rich mudflats and quiet river estuaries.

creatures.name: Estuarine fish
creatures.note: Migratory fish and salmon use the river mouths of Ronalindon to spawn, supporting local Elven fishing.

flora.description: Lush coastal evergreen stands, poplars, and damp understorey plants such as ferns and mosses.

plants.name: Maritime pines and poplars
plants.note: Stands of pines resilient to sea-sprays line the coast, transitioning to poplars and holly along the river banks.

people.description: A quiet, sparse population of Laiquendi and Falathrim Elves who lead simple, maritime-focused lives.

list.name: Elves of Ronalindon
list.description: Green-elves and sea-elves living in scattered coastal hamlets, engaged in fishing, woodcarving, and song.

land.name: Ronalindon Estuaries
land.description: Quiet river estuaries and tidal marshes emptying into the Gulf of Lhûn.
land.biome_hint: coastal wetland and river estuaries with high humidity and poplars

regions.notes: Ronalindon is a deeply spiritual and peaceful retreat for sea-yearning Elves before they depart from the Grey Havens. Eerily beautiful remnants of Beleriand, characterized by rugged coastal hills, fertile valleys, and evergreen forests. A light rain, drizzle, or ethereal fog falls on most days of the year, allowing mosses, ferns, firs, and spruce to thrive.
regions.elevation: average: 500 feet. lowest point: Felaigram (sea level at low tide, 2 fathoms below at high tide). highest point: Amon Draug 1,634 feet.
regions.administrative_organization: Círdan rules as a common Lord over the nobility of Forlindon and Harlindon, and his rule is acknowledged as a wise and peaceful one by all parties. Nobles live in pleasant and serene estates, sharing their bounty with their subjects.
regions.military: 2,000 Warriors and Sailors (Forlindon: 800 Warriors/Sailors, 800 Levy. Harlindon: 1,200 Sailors/Warriors, 800 Militia).
regions.symbol: Círdan's Device: A White Pelannun on a Blue Field

regions.name: Druwaith Laur
regions.description: Drúwaith Iaur, or Old Púkel-land, is an extremely remote, isolated, and obscure territory in northwestern Gondor, bordering Andrast to the west and the Westmarch to the east. Once claimed and nominally administered by Gondor, the region fell into extreme desertion and depopulation following the Great Plague of T.A. 1636. By T.A. 1640, no Dúnedain remain, leaving the wild, rugged, and uncultivated terrain to be ruled at a clan level by scattered tribes of Dunmen (Dunir) and native Drúedain (Wild Men).

climate.description: Druwaith Laur has a wet, maritime-temperate coastal climate heavily influenced by ocean air and storms coming off the Sea of Belegaer.
climate.temperature_pattern: Winters are mild with very rare frosts due to the moderating influence of the sea, while summers are cool, humid, and windy. Mean annual temperature ranges from 45°F to 55°F.
climate.precipitation_pattern: High annual precipitation of 45–60 inches, with frequent drizzle, heavy coastal rainstorms, and thick maritime fogs blanketting the hills.
climate.notes: The high humidity and frequent mists keep the wild soil damp and make overland travel disorienting to outsiders.

fauna.description: The wild, uncultivated hills support a variety of typical coastal and mountain wildlife, similar to neighboring Andrast.

creatures.name: Feral and forest mammals
creatures.note: Wolves, wild boars, feral goats on the rocky cliffs, and cathfiar (wild forest cats) are common throughout the unpeopled hills.

creatures.name: Venomous reptiles and insects
creatures.note: Poisonous snakes and spiders thrive in the damp hollows, marshes, and high grass margins of the coastal valleys.

flora.description: The vegetation is dominated by unmanaged coastal heathlands, rough marshes, and thick, mossy forest pockets.

plants.name: Coastal heath and gorse
plants.note: Exposed hills are carpeted with thick heather, gorse, and rugged shrubs that thrive in salty sea-spray.

plants.name: Damp ravines and woodland pockets
plants.note: Sheltered ravines and river valleys support dense stands of oak, ash, poplars, ferns, and thick mosses.

people.description: Entirely depopulated of Gondorian settlers; inhabited solely by native Drúedain and rustic Dunlending clans.

list.name: Wild Men of Drúwaith Iaur
list.description: Native Drúedain (Woses) who live primitive lives in the most remote, inaccessible mountain valleys and deep woods, completely avoiding contact with the outside world.

list.name: Dunir (Dunmen)
list.description: Scattered, pastoral tribes of Dunlending descent who herd livestock in the foothills and maintain small, family-level family groups.

land.name: Old Púkel-land wilderness
land.description: The rugged, roadless, and disorienting hills and valleys making up the core territory of Druwaith Laur.
land.biome_hint: wet maritime hills and uncultivated grasslands with dense mossy valleys

regions.notes: Drúwaith Iaur remains an unadministered 'domain of Wild Men' on Gondor's western border, viewed with anxiety by the settlers of the Westmarch.

regions.name: Druadan Forest
regions.description: The Drúadan Forest (S: 'Tawar-in-Drúedain') is an ancient, dense, and secretive oakwood situated in northeastern Gondor along the northern slopes of the White Mountains. Bordered by the beacon-hills of Eilenach and Nardol, this forest is the ancestral home of the Woses (Drúedain), a primeval, secretive race of master woodsmen who lead primitive, clan-based lives in hidden hollows and valleys completely apart from Gondorian civilization.

climate.description: Sheltered, temperate forest climate with high canopy cover that buffers temperature extremes and traps high humidity.
climate.temperature_pattern: Relatively mild winters and cool, shaded summers. Mean temperatures range from 45°F to 55°F, with the dense oak canopy preventing direct sunlight from scorching the forest floor.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Regular rainfall of 25–45 inches annually, supplemented by thick morning mists rising from the Drúnen River and the mountain glens.
climate.notes: The microclimate of the forest's interior is significantly damper and cooler than the exposed plains of Anórien to the north.

fauna.description: Rich temperate forest wildlife, including dense communities of songbirds, small mammals, and larger predators in the deep rocky ravines.

creatures.name: Woodland predators and mammals
creatures.note: Great brown bears, wild boars, large forest cats, and wolves hunt in the deeper, wilder sections of the forest.

creatures.name: Cave bats of Eilenach
creatures.note: Large colonies of bats roost in the limestone caves and old quarries, emerging at dusk to hunt insects over the forest canopy.

flora.description: Ancient mixed broadleaf and coniferous forest, dominated by massive oaks, with a highly diverse understorey of aromatic shrubs and wildflowers.

plants.name: Ancient Oaks and Pines
plants.note: Massive, old-growth oaks (Tawar) form the dominant canopy, mixed with wild pines on the higher rocky ridges.

plants.name: Aromatic shrubs and bright-lilies
plants.note: The understorey is rich in sweet-smelling herbs, wild berries, ferns, and glades filled with fragrant bright-lilies.

people.description: Inhabited by approximately 1,800 Woses (Drúedain) divided among more than 130 family-level clans, with occasional Gondorian quarrymen on the margins.

list.name: Woses (Drúedain of the Forest)
list.description: A primeval race of hunters and warriors ruled by Rhân-bun-Rhân, the High Drûghan. They are peerless woodsmen, crafty in forest lore, who speak an ancient, unpronounceable tongue. They use blowguns with poisoned darts and fiercely guard their sacred clearings.

list.name: Gondorian Quarrymen
list.description: Gondorian laborers who occasionally work the ancient limestone quarries (like Nardol Quarry) on the forest's northern margins.

land.name: Eilenach Glens and Stonewain Valley
land.description: A network of deep, hidden limestone ravines and narrow valleys where the Woses establish their hidden villages.
land.biome_hint: cool, shaded limestone ravines with dense undergrowth and secret trails

land.name: Drúadan Forest Quarries
land.description: Ancient stone quarries on the edge of the forest, once used to build Minas Anor but now largely overgrown and abandoned.
land.biome_hint: abandoned stone cuts and rock faces with overgrown ivy

regions.notes: The Woses no longer work in stone, but they maintain and revere the Pûkel-men stone statues carved by their ancestors, using them to guard their bounds.
regions.administrative_organization: On a clan level only. Shamans serve as the social and religious leaders of the communities.
regions.military: 400 Hunters and Warriors.

regions.name: Dorwinion
regions.description: Dorwinion, or the 'Wineland,' is a wealthy and fertile country located along the northwest coast of the Sea of Rhûn and the lower reaches of the swift River Celduin and the rushing Uldona. Known throughout Middle-earth for producing the finest wines, its culture is dominated by the Dorwinrim, a hardy, adaptable, and highly diplomatic merchant people. Their capital, Shrel-Kain, is the largest port on the sea and serves as the mercantile and diplomatic hub of the entire region.

climate.description: Dorwinion has a moderate, humid, and warm-temperate climate buffered by the massive thermal mass of the Sea of Rhûn.
climate.temperature_pattern: Winters are cool and damp but rarely severe, with mean temperatures around 50–60°F. Summers are long, warm, and highly sunny, providing the perfect growing season for extensive viticulture.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Abundant annual precipitation of 35–50 inches, falling as frequent light spring rains and heavy morning mists from the Celduin and Uldona valleys.
climate.notes: The thermal mass of the Sea of Rhûn prevents early autumn frosts, protecting the grape harvest.

fauna.description: Rich riverine, wetland, and estuarine wildlife typical of fertile, low-altitude valleys.

creatures.name: River and estuary species
creatures.note: Black minks, river otters, and cold-water fish like pike thrive in the Celduin. Giant freshwater snails (mabelmaikli) and leeches are found in the muddy shallows.

creatures.name: Waterfowl and forest birds
creatures.note: Waterfowl such as ducks, geese, and wild birds like black swans, crebain, and ravens nest along the river margins.

flora.description: Famous for its vast, terrace-cultivated agricultural estates, transitioning to mixed woodlands on surrounding hills.

plants.name: Vast grapevines and olive groves
plants.note: Sprawling, meticulously managed terraced vineyards producing the finest wines in Middle-earth, alongside groves of olives, wheat fields, and pomegranate trees.

plants.name: Riparian woodlands
plants.note: Dense willow, alder, poplar, and oak groves line the banks of the Celduin and its smaller tributaries.

people.description: Inhabited by the Dorwinrim, a sophisticated, trade-focused Dunadan-allied people who wear bright, gaily-colored clothes and are renowned as shrewd bargainers.

list.name: Dorwinrim (Wine-merchants)
list.description: Hardy and adaptable merchants, courageous sailors, and skilled diplomats who operate forts along the Celduin to protect trade caravans from Easterling raiders.

land.name: Celduin River Valley
land.description: The fertile life-blood of Dorwinion, snaking over 800 miles and snaking with smaller streams like Angren and Athorn.
land.biome_hint: humid river valley with intensive vineyards and deciduous tree lines

land.name: Shrel-Kain Port
land.description: The grand capital of Dorwinion and largest port on the Sea of Rhûn, situated upriver from the Celduin's mouth.
land.biome_hint: maritime merchant port with heavy ship traffic and grand stone markets

regions.notes: Dorwinion's wine trade reaches as far west as Thranduil's halls, using large river-rafts to navigate the Running River.

regions.name: Eothraim
regions.description: The territory of the Eothraim comprises the vast, fertile southern plains and rolling grasslands of southern Dor Rhunen, southwest of the Eorstan Hills and extending west toward Greenwood the Great. Watered by the Warwater and Surubeki rivers, this lush environment is home to the Eothraim—fierce, honest, and courageous horse-masters who live as semi-nomadic herders and hunters.

climate.description: Continental-temperate climate with warm, pleasant summers and cold, wind-swept winters.
climate.temperature_pattern: Mean annual temperatures range between 45°F and 55°F. Summers are warm and breezy (70–80°F), while winters bring freezing, snow-blown winds from the northeast across the Sea of Rhûn.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Moderate annual precipitation of 20–35 inches, characterized by sudden, intense spring downpours and light winter snow.
climate.notes: The open grasslands are highly exposed, meaning winter blizzards can sweep the plains unhindered.

fauna.description: The endless grasslands are dominated by large herds of wild horses and unique plains herbivores.

creatures.name: Wild horses of the plains
creatures.note: Superb, swift, and highly spirited horse herds that roam free across the grasslands, providing the breeding stock for the Eothraim's legendary mounts.

creatures.name: Culcarnix herds
creatures.note: Large, docile, and woolly herd-beasts native to the southern pastures, prized by plains nomads for their meat, hides, and milk.

creatures.name: Plains predators
creatures.note: Wolves, feral dogs, and marsh mastiffs (merisc tyka) roam the tall grasses and river deltas.

flora.description: A rolling sea of tall grasses, fertile soil, and scattered riverine vegetation, said to have been blessed by the ancient passage of the Entwives.

plants.name: Thick plains grasses
plants.note: Lush, highly nutritious wild wheat, rye, and sweet plains grasses that cover the rolling hills for miles in every direction.

plants.name: Riparian willow and poplar
plants.note: Scattered groves of poplars and willow trees grow in the humid valleys of the Warwater and Surubeki rivers.

people.description: Inhabited by the Eothraim, six proud and honest horse-tribes who value courage above all else and are fast friends with the Dorwinrim.

list.name: Eothraim (Horse-lords)
list.description: Superb equestrian warriors, semi-nomadic herders, and hunters who move between seasonal pastures and maintain a disunified but highly honorable society.

land.name: Southern Rhûn plains
land.description: The fertile, vast rolling grasslands watered by the turbid Warwater and the clear, pebble-strewn Surubeki.
land.biome_hint: lush grassland plains with rolling hills and scattered shrub river corridors

regions.notes: The Eothraim represent the cultural ancestors of the Rohirrim, sharing their deep connection to horses, wind, and honor.
regions.administrative_organization: Clan-based. Leaders usually hereditary, but sometimes elected by male warriors. Eothraim divided among six tribes, Gramuz among four; land held by family groups.
regions.military: 800 Eothraim Cavalry. 1,200 Eothraim Levy Cavalry. 75 Gramuz Warriors. 150 Gramuz Levy Warriors.
regions.symbol: Eothrain: White Horse on a Green Field. Gramuz: A Golden Sheaf of Grain on an Umber Field.

regions.name: Gramuz
regions.description: Gramuz is a rugged northern wilderland region of Dor Rhunen, situated north of Dorwinion and south of the Iron Hills. It is characterized by undulating lowlands, rolling hills, and scattered mixed woodlands. It is a harsh frontier territory populated by semi-nomadic Easterling clans and hardy Northmen who struggle for survival against the bitter cold winds blowing from the northeast Sea of Rhûn.

climate.description: Continental sub-arctic to cool-temperate climate, heavily impacted by the freezing, biting winds of the Sea of Rhûn.
climate.temperature_pattern: Short, cool summers and long, dark, and severe winters with frequent snowstorms. Mean annual temperature is 35–45°F.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Plentiful winter snow and moderate summer rain (20-30 inches), with thick frost.
climate.notes: Bitter cold winds from the northeast sweep across the open inland sea, intensifying winters in the Gramuz wilderlands.

fauna.description: Colder woodland and upland species, including apex mammalian predators.

creatures.name: Upland predators
creatures.note: Dire wolves, grey wolves, black bears, and giant martens thrive in the rugged woodlands and foothills.

creatures.name: Woodland birds
creatures.note: Crebain, gorcrows, and ravens nest in the scattered evergreen stands, alongside black minks in river rapids.

flora.description: Undulating lowlands of mixed broadleaf and coniferous woodlands, transitioning to rugged moorland and tundra to the north.

plants.name: Mixed broadleaf and conifers
plants.note: Gloomy pines, spruce, larch, sturdy oaks, and beeches grow in scattered woodlands across the rolling hills.

people.description: A melting pot of hardy Gramuz Northmen tribes and semi-nomadic Easterlings (Logath, Sagath, Odhriag).

list.name: Gramuz Northmen
list.description: Brave, resilient foresters and horse-herders with strong warrior traditions.

list.name: Sagath & Logath (Wain-easterlings)
list.description: Nomadic horsemen and chariot-riders who frequent the pastures.

land.name: Wilderlands of Dor Rhunen
land.description: Undulating lowlands and scattered forests lying north of the southern plains and south of the Iron Hills.
land.biome_hint: rolling plains and scattered pine‑oak forests with freezing winds

regions.notes: Gramuz forms a vital buffer zone between the Elven woodlands of Taur Romen and the Dwarven mines of Eorstan.
regions.administrative_organization: Clan-based. Leaders usually hereditary, but sometimes elected by male warriors. Eothraim divided among six tribes, Gramuz among four; land held by family groups.
regions.military: 800 Eothraim Cavalry. 1,200 Eothraim Levy Cavalry. 75 Gramuz Warriors. 150 Gramuz Levy Warriors.
regions.symbol: Eothrain: White Horse on a Green Field. Gramuz: A Golden Sheaf of Grain on an Umber Field.

regions.name: Dor Rhunen
regions.description: Dor Rhunen, the 'Land of the East,' comprises the semi-arid, vast southern plains and rolling grasslands surrounding the southern shores of the Sea of Rhûn. Extending southwest toward the desolate Battle Plain of Dagorlad and bordered to the south by the ash-choked fences of Mordor, this is a harsh, wind-swept buffer territory. It is heavily influenced by the cultures of Rohan and the Wold to the west, but dominated by exceptionally brutal nomadic Easterling tribes, such as the Variags, Nuriags, and Asdriags, who frequently raid the trade caravans.

climate.description: Continental semi-arid grassland climate with extreme temperature swings, heavily influenced by dry winds from the southern deserts and cold, bitter squalls from the Sea of Rhûn.
climate.temperature_pattern: Very hot, dry summers where temperatures easily exceed 85°F on the open steppes, and freezing, harsh winters with severe frost and biting winds. Mean annual temperature is 45–55°F.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Low to moderate annual precipitation of 15–25 inches, falling mostly as sparse winter snow and sudden, violent summer thunderstorms.
climate.notes: The open grasslands are highly exposed, leaving them vulnerable to extreme temperature swings and sudden windstorms.

fauna.description: Robust, steppe-adapted animals and dangerous predators, with a strong presence of dark creatures wandering from Mordor and Dagorlad.

creatures.name: Steppe predators and scavengers
creatures.note: Grey wolves, dire wolves, wild horses, marsh mastiffs (merisc tyka), and dangerous carrion-eaters like gorcrows, crebain, and ravens.

creatures.name: Dark creatures
creatures.note: Orcs, trolls, and undead wander from the southern wastes of Mordor and the Battle Plain of Dagorlad.

flora.description: Dry, short-grass steppe and rough scrublands, with vegetation becoming sparse and stunted as it transitions south toward Dagorlad and Khand.

plants.name: Steppe grasses and scrub
plants.note: Stiff feather-grasses, wild rye, hardy sagebrush, and thorny shrubs that survive in dry, wind-swept soils.

people.description: A highly volatile frontier populated by warlike nomadic horsemen, brutal Easterling tribes, and occasional raiding Orc bands.

list.name: Variags and Nuriags
list.description: Brutal, aggressive Easterling tribes who ride swift steeds and frequently harass borders and trade routes.

list.name: Asdriags
list.description: A savage, wain-riding Easterling people who wander the grasslands in search of plunder.

list.name: Wandering Eothraim
list.description: Semi-nomadic herders and horse-masters who clash with the Easterling tribes over pasture lands.

land.name: Southern Dor Rhunen Grasslands
land.description: The dry, vast plains transitioning into the desolation of Dagorlad to the west and Mordor to the south.
land.biome_hint: semi-arid dry grasslands with sparse thorny scrub and high exposure

regions.notes: Dor Rhunen serves as a dangerous, lawless transition zone connecting the western lands of Rhovanion with the eastern expanses of Khand.

regions.name: Mordor
regions.description: Mordor, the Black Land, is the volcanic plateau realm of the Dark Lord Sauron, walled in on three sides by the formidable mountain chains of Ephel Dúath and Ered Lithui. The plateau is arid, rugged, and heavily Orc-infested, serving as a fortress-kingdom of evil. Bedrock consists of black volcanic basalt giving the region its characteristic black, scorched appearance.
regions.elevation: average: 3,100 feet. lowest point: Morannon 850 feet. highest point: Orod Rauveleg 9,032 feet.

climate.description: Continental volcanic-arid climate with extreme, searing heat, choking sulfur vapors, and almost no rain. Mean annual temperature: 80-90° F. Average low: Girithon 25° F. Average high: Ivanneth 120° F. Precipitation: 5-20 inches annually.
climate.temperature_pattern: Searing summer highs on the Gorgoroth plains reach up to 120°F, while winters drop to a mild 25°F under dark ash-laden skies.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Extremely dry with rare rainfall (5–20 inches), evaporating almost instantly on the volcanic slopes.
climate.notes: The active furnace of Mount Doom (Orodruin) regularly spews toxic soot and ash-clouds that blanket the skies of Gorgoroth.

fauna.description: Barren of pleasant wildlife; inhabited primarily by corrupted beasts and monsters.

creatures.name: Corrupted predators
creatures.note: Giant spiders, fell beasts, and carrion-eating black crebain nest in the black volcanic spires.

flora.description: Entirely barren and scorched in the Gorgoroth region, with stunted vegetation existing only on the margins.

plants.name: Scrub and ash-weeds
plants.note: A few scrawny thorny shrubs, mosses, and ash-tolerant weeds grow in deep rocky fissures.

people.description: Warlike populations of Orcs, Trolls, Half-Orcs, and dark Men loyal to Sauron.

list.name: Sauron's Armies
list.description: Huge armies of Orcs, Olog-hai (trolls), and Easterling/Haradrim auxiliaries garrisoning the black towers.

land.name: Gorgoroth Plain
land.description: A bare, wind-swept plain of black basalt and active volcanic vents.
land.biome_hint: scorched volcanic plateau with basalt rock faces and ash-clouds

regions.notes: Mordor remains an impenetrable natural fortress, viewed with terror by all Free Peoples.
regions.administrative_organization: Gondor's military holds the only legitimate and stable authority in Mordor. Command is divided into three districts, each centered around one of the citadels in the Ephel Dúath. This leaves most of Gorgoroth and all of Nurn unadministered, but the risks are too great to act otherwise.
regions.military: Durthang: 200 Gondorian Soldiers. Cirith Ungol: 100 Gondorian Soldiers. Cirith Gorgor: 300 Gondorian Soldiers. Conquered Host: 11,000 Orc Warriors. 2,170 Uruk-hai. 750 Warg-riders. 100 Trolls.

regions.name: Nurn
regions.description: Nurn is the more fertile, semi-arid southern province of Mordor surrounding the highly saline Lake Nurnen. Enriched by volcanic soils and watered by mountain streams, Nurn serves as the slave-worked breadbasket feeding the massive armies of Sauron.
regions.elevation: 2,600 feet.

climate.description: Hot, semi-arid plains climate with high evaporation. Mean annual temperature: 70-80° F. Average low: Girithon 30° F. Average high: Ivanneth 100° F. Precipitation: 10-20 inches annually.
climate.temperature_pattern: Warm throughout the year with very hot summers (90–100°F) and mild winters.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Low annual rainfall (10–20 inches) heavily supplemented by run-off from the surrounding mountain ranges.
climate.notes: The humidity is higher near the shores of Lake Nurnen, but the air remains hazy with volcanic dust.

fauna.description: Unlike Gorgoroth, Nurn supports basic wildlife and aquatic birds.

creatures.name: Wading birds of Nurnen
creatures.note: Large wading birds stand on the shallow shoals of Lake Nurnen, and some fish survive in its brackish waters.

flora.description: Volcanic-enriched plains of wild grains, grasses, and slave-cultivated fields of wheat and barley.

plants.name: Coarse grasses and crops
plants.note: Wide fields of wild rye, coarse grasses, and agricultural grains irrigated by fresh mountain runoff.

people.description: Populated by tens of thousands of slaves and their Orc taskmasters.

list.name: Slaves of Nurn
list.description: Captive Men from the East and South forced to work the vast agricultural fields.

land.name: Plains of Nurnen
land.description: Fertile volcanic plains irrigated by rivers coming off the Ephel Dúath.
land.biome_hint: semi-arid volcanic plains with intensive grain farming

regions.notes: Lake Nurnen is a bitter, saline, and chemical-heavy inland sea with no natural outlet.

regions.name: Nan Anduin
regions.description: Nan Anduin is the long, wide, and highly fertile alluvial valley through which the Great River Anduin flows between the Misty Mountains and Mirkwood. It contains some of the richest soil in Middle-earth, supporting scattered communities of Beornings, Woodmen, and Northman pioneers.
regions.elevation: average: 900 feet. lowest point: Mouth of Celebrant 650 feet. highest point: Amon Thyryr 8,002 feet.

climate.description: Temperate continental river-valley climate with regular rainfall and snowy winters. Mean annual temperature: 45-55° F. Average low: Nínui 5° F. Average high: Úrui 90° F. Precipitation: 25-45 inches annually.
climate.temperature_pattern: Pleasant, warm summers (80–90°F) and cold, snow-bound winters.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Moderate to high annual precipitation (25–45 inches) spread across the seasons, with regular spring rains.
climate.notes: Water running off the Hithaeglir provides ample fresh water and keeps the alluvial soil moist.

fauna.description: Rich temperate riverine and woodland wildlife, including large mammalian herds and birds.

creatures.name: Forest and river mammals
creatures.note: Deer, wolves, badgers, river otters, and beavers are common along the banks and secondary valleys.

flora.description: Lush alluvial plains, deciduous woodlands, and agricultural crops.

plants.name: Grasslands and broadleaves
plants.note: Thick wild grains, clover, sweet grasses, and copses of willow, alder, poplar, and elm.

people.description: Home to Beornings (Beijabar), Woodmen, and pastoral Northmen.

list.name: Beornings
list.description: Hardy, strong-willed bear-folk who live in close symbiosis with animals and guard the Carrock.

land.name: Anduin Floodplains
land.description: The fertile, wide flat lands bordering the Great River.
land.biome_hint: fertile alluvial plains with willow groves and wheat fields

regions.notes: The Anduin is the life-blood of Rhovanion, serving as a natural trade corridor and barrier.
regions.political_organization: Independent settlements, each ruled by an elite warrior lord.
regions.administrative_organization: Each clan strives for selfreliance, meeting with the others only for occasional trade and in times of crisis. Within the clan, all power rests with the clan chief, a warrior of noble lineage (frequently the descendant of the previous leader) chosen by all adult males.
regions.military: 100 Beornings. 60 Burgwards of Maethelburg. 900 Militia (3-4 weeks to raise, organized by clan). 135 Eagles (allies).
regions.products: Grain, hides, cattle, honey, amber, tin.

regions.name: Fornost
regions.description: Fornost Erain, the 'Norbury of the Kings', was once the grand, fortified northern capital of the kingdom of Arthedain, located at the northern end of the North Downs. Now a silent, massive ruin known as Deadmen's Dike, it is haunted by ghosts, wraiths, and the memories of the fallen Dúnedain. The ruins are characterized by crumbling stone ramparts, collapsed halls, and overgrown ditches.
regions.elevation: average: 1,200 feet.

climate.description: Cold, windy, and damp temperate climate with long, frosty winters and brief, pleasant summers. Mean annual temperature: 40-50° F. Average annual precipitation: 35-45 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Winters are exceptionally cold and snow-prone, with winds blowing down from the northern wastes.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Precipitation falls heavily as winter snow and damp spring rains.
climate.notes: The ruins are often shrouded in a thick, chilling fog that rolls in from the surrounding downs.

fauna.description: Lizards, crows, bats, and wolves live among the ruins. Evil wargs and wraiths guard the deeper vaults.

creatures.name: Ruins scavengers
creatures.note: Rats, owls, and crows nest in the collapsed watchtowers.

flora.description: Overgrown with wild grasses, briars, ivy, and nettles.

plants.name: Nettles and briars
plants.note: Thorny vines and thick weeds blanket the ancient flagstones.

people.description: Deserted by civilized folk; only rangers and occasional tomb-robbers pass through.

list.name: Rangers of the North
list.description: Heirs of Arthedain who watch the ruins to prevent evil forces from occupying them.

land.name: Deadmen's Dike
land.description: The massive defensive earthworks and stone foundations of the ruined city.
land.biome_hint: fortified ruins atop wind-swept downs with high exposure

regions.notes: Fornost remains a place of dread, avoided by locals due to rumors of active dark spirits.
regions.political_organization: Ruins; nominally under the protection of the Rangers of the North.
rulers: Marl Tarma, Captain of the Rangers
regions.administrative_organization: None.
regions.military: Occasional Ranger patrols.
regions.products: None (relics and ancient treasures of Arnor).

regions.name: Harithilien
regions.supplement: Tolkien (LotR) with Ithilien / Gondor regional material
regions.description: Harithilien is South Ithilien, a distinct, fertile, and beautiful region bordering Harondor and Mordor. It is characterized by volcanic soils enriched by the Ephel Dúath, featuring ancient ruined estates of Gondor, hidden ranger outposts, and lush vegetation.

lotr_refs: The Return of the King, Book VI

regions.elevation: average: 600 feet.

climate.description: Warm, sunny, and pleasant Mediterranean climate moderated by moist sea-winds. Mean annual temperature: 55-65° F. Average annual precipitation: 35-45 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Mild, short winters and warm, beautiful summers.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Adequate winter rains with dry, sunny summers.
climate.notes: Orographic rainfall along the western slopes of the Ephel Dúath keeps the valley green and fertile.

fauna.description: Rich temperate and sub-tropical wildlife, including wild boars, mountain goats, and deer.

creatures.name: Steppe and hill wildlife
creatures.note: Badgers, wild horses, and birds of prey nest in the valleys.

flora.description: Lush evergreen forests, olive groves, wild herbs, and flowering shrubs.

plants.name: Bay and laurel
plants.note: Aromatic shrubs and laurels grow in abundance in the moist ravines.

people.description: Populated by rangers and skirmishing Gondor/Mordor soldiers.

list.name: Rangers of Ithilien
list.description: Gondorian rangers who launch stealth raids against Sauron's forces.

land.name: Ephel Dúath Valleys
land.description: The green, deep valleys on the western face of the Mountains of Shadow.
land.biome_hint: semi-arid to dry upland with volcanic-soil fields and scrub

regions.notes: Harithilien is highly contested and frequently raided by Haradrim and Orcs marching north.
regions.administrative_organization: Military governorship.
regions.military: Ranger squads.

regions.name: Moria
regions.description: Moria, or Khazad-dûm, is the ancient and legendary underground Dwarf-city of the House of Durin, carved deep beneath the Misty Mountains. Now a vast, dark abyss occupied by Orcs, Cave-Trolls, and the shadow of the Balrog.

climate.description: Completely subterranean; damp, cold, and dark, with constant drafts blowing through the great shafts. Average temperature: 40-50° F.
climate.temperature_pattern: Damp and stable year-round inside the deeper caverns.
climate.precipitation_pattern: No precipitation; damp walls and underground rivers like the Silverlode flow from the snowpack above.
climate.notes: Drafts and ventilation shafts create howling wind corridors in the upper halls.

fauna.description: Cave-trolls, orcs, giant bats, and deep-dwelling horrors from the dawn of time.

creatures.name: Cave-Trolls
creatures.note: Huge, slow-witted, and stone-skinned trolls that dwell in the dark shafts.

flora.description: Limited to subterranean fungi, molds, and luminescent lichens.

plants.name: Cave Fungi
plants.note: Huge bioluminescent fungi grow in the damp, lower vaults.

people.description: Currently occupied by the Orc-hordes of the Misty Mountains and a few isolated dwarven parties.

list.name: Orcs of Khazad-dûm
list.description: Sauron's servants who plunder the ancient dwarven vaults.

land.name: Deeps of Moria
land.description: The bottomless mines and subterranean pits where mithril is found.
land.biome_hint: subterranean mine-shafts and stone bridges over deep abysses

regions.notes: Moria is a place of absolute darkness, feared by Elves and Men alike.
rulers: Falin, Commander of the Mansion Watch
regions.administrative_organization: The King holds the Dwarfmansion in the name of the House of Durin.
regions.military: Mansion Watch and Weapon Host.
regions.products: Precious metals, gems, finished products, Mithril.
regions.symbol: Durin's Folk: Seven Stars in a Triangular Formation

regions.name: Numeriador
regions.description: Numeriador is the cold, rugged, and wind-swept northern plateau of Eriador, lying north of the Hills of Evendim. It is a sparsely populated, rolling wilderness of grasses and stunted shrubs, once part of the kingdom of Arnor.
regions.elevation: average: 1,500 feet.

climate.description: Sub-arctic, dry, and cold climate with harsh, freezing winters and short, cool summers. Mean annual temperature: 35-45° F. Average annual precipitation: 15-25 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Winters are extremely harsh, with freezing winds blowing off the Ice Bay of Forochel.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Low annual precipitation, mostly falling as dry, powdery snow.
climate.notes: Highly exposed plains vulnerable to sudden, violent blizzards.

fauna.description: Grey wolves, arctic foxes, mountain goats, and herds of wild horses.

creatures.name: Grey wolves
creatures.note: Large packs hunt deer and wild horses across the open plains.

flora.description: Sparse dwarf-willows, heather, wild rye, and hardy mosses.

plants.name: Tundra grasses
plants.note: Dry, stiff grasses that can survive frost and deep snow.

people.description: Sparsely populated by wandering hillmen and isolated shepherds.

list.name: Hillmen shepherds
list.description: Hardy nomadic herders who graze their sheep in the southern valleys.

land.name: Numeriador Steppes
land.description: The open, flat, and cold grasslands extending north toward Forochel.
land.biome_hint: semi-arid cold steppe with sparse thorny scrub and high exposure

regions.notes: Numeriador acts as a vast, lawless buffer zone between Arnor and the frozen north.
regions.administrative_organization: None.
regions.military: None.

regions.name: Old Forest
regions.supplement: Tolkien (LotR) with Shire / Eriador regional material
regions.description: The Old Forest is an ancient, sentient, and deeply shadowed woodland bordering the eastern Shire. Known for its hostile, moving trees and gnarled roots, it is ruled by the mysterious Old Man Willow and protected by Tom Bombadil.

lotr_refs: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book I (chapter 6: 'The Old Forest')

regions.elevation: average: 600 feet.

climate.description: Damp, stagnant, and humid forest climate, heavily shadowed by the dense canopy. Mean annual temperature: 48-55° F. Average annual precipitation: 35-45 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Mild and shaded summers, damp and foggy winters.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Evenly distributed rain, with heavy ground mist and stagnant humidity.
climate.notes: The trees themselves seem to trap air and light, creating a heavy, sleepy atmosphere.

fauna.description: Large badgers, owls, bats, and other woodland creatures. The forest itself is sentient and hostile.

creatures.name: Sentient trees
creatures.note: The trees are active, moving their branches to trap intruders and block paths.

flora.description: Ancient oaks, maples, elms, creeping ivy, and dense layers of thick moss.

plants.name: Gnarled oaks
plants.note: Ancient, twisted trees that actively murmur and whisper to one another.

people.description: Uninhabited by mortals; Hobbits fear to enter, and only Tom Bombadil dwells on its borders.

list.name: Tom Bombadil
list.description: The oldest being, who sings songs to calm the angry trees of the forest.

land.name: Withywindle Valley
land.description: The dark, deep river valley at the heart of the forest, where Old Man Willow lies.
land.biome_hint: dense primeval forest with gnarled roots, stagnant bogs, and moving trees

regions.notes: The Old Forest is incredibly dangerous; pathways shift, and the trees actively conspire against travelers.
regions.administrative_organization: None.
regions.military: The forest itself is its own defense.

regions.name: Parth Celebrant
regions.supplement: Rohan: The Land of the Horse-Lords
regions.description: Parth Celebrant, the Field of Celebrant, is a wide, rolling grassy plain nestled between the Great River Anduin and the Limlight. It is famous as the historical battleground where Eorl the Young led the Éothéod to save the armies of Gondor, leading to the gift of Rohan.
regions.elevation: average: 800 feet.

climate.description: Temperate continental climate with warm summers and cold, snowy winters. Mean annual temperature: 45-55° F. Average annual precipitation: 25-35 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Winters are chilly with occasional blizzards, while summers are bright, warm, and windy.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Moderate rainfall, mostly in spring, with winter snowpack.
climate.notes: Constant winds sweep across the open, flat grasslands.

fauna.description: Wild horses, birds of prey, grey wolves, and grazing herd beasts.

creatures.name: Wild horses
creatures.note: Legendary herds of swift, powerful steeds roam the plains.

flora.description: Rich, waist-high grasses, clover, and scattered maple and elm groves along the riverbanks.

plants.name: Steppe grasses
plants.note: Thick, nutrient-rich grasses that support major herds.

people.description: Inhabited by Rohirrim horsemen and patrols from Gondor/Rohan.

list.name: Rohirrim patrols
list.description: Mounted warriors who keep watch on the northern borders against Orc raids.

land.name: Celebrant Plain
land.description: The historic, rolling battlefield between the rivers.
land.biome_hint: rolling grasslands and alluvial fields with high exposure

regions.notes: Parth Celebrant remains a highly revered historic site, commemorating the ancient alliance between Rohan and Gondor.
regions.political_organization: Territory of the Kingdom of Rohan.
regions.administrative_organization: Military border district.
regions.population: Sparsely populated (military outposts and nomadic herders).
regions.military: Mounted Eoreds (patrols).
regions.symbol: White Horse on a Green Field

regions.name: Ronen-in-Anduin
regions.description: Ronen-in-Anduin is the eastern floodplains and low rolling hills bordering the Great River Anduin as it passes through the territory of Rhovanion. It represents a transition zone between the river valleys and the wide eastern steppes.
regions.elevation: average: 1,000 feet.

climate.description: Temperate continental steppe climate with hot summers and freezing, wind-swept winters. Mean annual temperature: 45-55° F. Average annual precipitation: 20-30 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Extreme temperature swings, with summers reaching 90°F and winters dropping below zero.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Low to moderate rainfall, mostly in spring, with light winter snow.
climate.notes: Regular high-velocity winds sweep across the open plains from the east.

fauna.description: Wild dogs, jackals, antelopes, and wolves. Large numbers of waterfowl nest along the marshy river margins.

creatures.name: Antelopes and wild cattle
creatures.note: Large herds graze the open steppe, preyed upon by jackals and wolves.

flora.description: Stiff prairie grasses, feather-grass, wild rye, and thick willow groves on the river banks.

plants.name: Prairie grasses
plants.note: Feather-grass and wild rye blanket the rolling, dry plains.

people.description: Inhabited by scattered Northmen herders and wandering Easterling scouts.

list.name: Northmen pastoralists
list.description: Semi-nomadic herders who graze their cattle along the riverbanks.

land.name: Anduin Margins
land.description: The low, rolling floodplains bordering the river.
land.biome_hint: river floodplains merging into semi-arid grassy steppe

regions.notes: Ronen-in-Anduin is highly vulnerable to raids from Easterlings crossing the River Running.
regions.administrative_organization: Clan-based.
regions.military: Local clan militia.

regions.name: Tower Hills
regions.supplement: Tolkien (LotR) with Lindon / Shire regional material
regions.description: The Tower Hills, or Emyn Beraid, are a range of green, rolling hills located west of the Shire. On their highest crests stand three ancient Elven-towers built by Gil-galad, where the Elendil Stone (palantír) was guarded for millennia.

lotr_refs: The Fellowship of the Ring, Prologue 1
The Silmarillion, Of the Rings of Power and the Third Age

regions.elevation: average: 800 feet.

climate.description: Oceanic, pleasant, and mild climate strongly moderated by the sea breezes of Lindon. Mean annual temperature: 50-58° F. Average annual precipitation: 35-45 inches.
climate.temperature_pattern: Mild winters with rare frost, and cool, bright, and beautiful summers.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Abundant, gentle rainfall evenly spread throughout the year.
climate.notes: Frequent morning mists and light drizzle give the hills an ethereal, serene quality.

fauna.description: Deer, rabbits, foxes, and numerous songbirds. Protected from wolves and dark beasts by Elven guards.

creatures.name: Forest deer
creatures.note: Red deer graze peacefully in the secluded valleys between the hills.

flora.description: Green rolling pastures, fields of heather, and beautiful groves of silver birch and beech.

plants.name: Silver birch
plants.note: Beautiful white-barked birch trees grow in lovely groves on the slopes.

people.description: Inhabited by high elves of Lindon and occasional Hobbit travelers.

list.name: Elves of Mithlond
list.description: High Elves who keep watch over the Elostirion tower and the road to the west.

land.name: Emyn Beraid Crests
land.description: The high chalk ridges where the three ancient towers stand.
land.biome_hint: rolling green chalk downs with heather fields and birch groves

regions.notes: The Tower Hills are a tranquil, holy sanctuary where the Elves preserve ancient memories of the First Age.
regions.political_organization: Autonomous territory under the protection of Lindon.
regions.administrative_organization: None.
regions.military: Elven archer squads.
regions.symbol: Círdan's Device: A White Pelannun on a Blue Field

regions.name: Belegaer sea
regions.description: The Belegaer, or Great Sea, is the vast ocean separating Middle-earth (Endor) from the Undying Lands of Aman in the far West. In the coastal domains of Lindon, Arnor, and Gondor, it comprises key areas such as the Gulf of Lhûn (Nen Lhûn) and the Bay of Belfalas (Nen Belfalas). The deep gulf of Nen Lhûn, formed in the cataclysms of the First Age, serves as the calm and misty harbor for the Elven ships sailing from Mithlond, rich in salmon, lobsters, and shellfish. Further south, the vast Bay of Belfalas rests on Endor's continental shelf, driven by the warm, counterclockwise Southron Stream which collides with the cold Great Stream off the Cape of Andrast, creating turbulent, dangerous waters home to sharks, grey sperm whales, and giant squids.
regions.type: Remnants of a Drowned Land / Great Sea
regions.elevation: average: Sea level. deepest point: 300 fathoms.

climate.description: Highly variable marine climate, ranging from cool-temperate and misty in the north (Gulf of Lhûn) to warm, Mediterranean, and maritime in the south (Bay of Belfalas).
climate.temperature_pattern: Average temperatures in the north range from 20°F to 80°F, while the southern Bay of Belfalas remains mild and warm, averaging 50-60°F year-round.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Abundant precipitation of 40-60 inches annually, falling as light drizzle, coastal fog, and heavy seasonal autumn downpours.
climate.notes: Subject to sudden, violent autumn storms and hurricanes blowing in from the deep ocean.

fauna.description: Abundant marine life and dangerous ocean predators.

creatures.name: Marine Wildlife
creatures.note: Salmon, lobsters, shellfish, and large schools of coastal fish.

creatures.name: Ocean Predators
creatures.note: Sharks, grey sperm whales, and legendary giant squids that dwell in the deep continental shelf.

flora.description: Aquatic flora, giant kelp forests, and coastal dune grasses.

plants.name: Kelp and Seaweed
plants.note: Dense underwater kelp forests that shelter diverse fish species.

people.description: Traversed primarily by Elven ships sailing West, Gondorian merchants, and Haradrim/Corsair raiders.

list.name: Elves of the Grey Havens
list.description: Elven mariners sailing the Sunset-rounders into the Undying West.

list.name: Corsairs of Umbar
list.description: Ruthless pirate fleets raiding Gondor's merchant lanes.

land.name: Gulf of Lhûn
land.description: The deep, misty gulf cutting through the Blue Mountains.
land.biome_hint: deep gulf with rocky coastal cliffs and heavy fog

land.name: Bay of Belfalas
land.description: The great warm-water bay washing the southern shores of Gondor.
land.biome_hint: warm bay with sandy beaches and seasonal hurricanes

regions.notes: A gateway to the Undying Lands, which can only be reached by Elven ships sailing along the Straight Road.
regions.political_organization: Ungoverned waters; patrolled by Círdan's mariners in the north and the fleets of Gondor and Umbar in the south.
regions.administrative_organization: None.
regions.military: Elven guard ships, Gondor fleet squads, Corsair war-dromunds.

regions.name: En Udanoriath
regions.description: En Udanoriath, meaning the 'No-Man's-Land' or the 'Dwelling-less Waste', is a bleak, barren, and wind-scarred frontier zone located between the southern border of Angmar and the northern boundaries of Arthedain (near the North Downs). This cold highland waste is a desolate, rocky steppe plagued by bitter northern winds and constant military skirmishes. It serves as a buffer and raiding corridor where the Witch-king's patrols clash with Arnor's remnants, overseen by spy-towers like Mindil Cargash.
regions.elevation: average: 1,500 - 2,500 feet.

climate.description: Cold, dry, and wind-swept highland climate with extreme winters and brief, chilly summers.
climate.temperature_pattern: Average temperatures remain below freezing for most of the year, with bitter frost-bite winds and sudden drops down to -20°F.
climate.precipitation_pattern: Low precipitation of 10-20 inches annually, mostly as dry, wind-blown snow.
climate.notes: Highly unstable weather with regular blizzards rolling down from the northern wastes.

fauna.description: Hardy scavengers and dangerous predators trained or controlled by Angmar.

creatures.name: Predators of the waste
creatures.note: Grey wolves, dire wolves, and large bats used as scouts.

creatures.name: Dark forces
creatures.note: Orc patrols, warg-riders, and occasional stone-trolls hunting for food.

flora.description: Minimal, stunted tundra vegetation and hardy mosses.

plants.name: Wasteland lichen
plants.note: Black lichens and tough heather that cling to the frost-shattered rocks.

people.description: Entirely devoid of permanent settlements; traversed only by soldiers, orc warbands, and Dunlending mercenaries.

list.name: Angmarean Patrols
